title: Add an example for skopeo inspect --override-os
body:
tl;dr - Is there a supported way to tell skopeo inspect to use a specific architecture and os? I don't see anything in `skopeo inspect --help` that points towards it.

---

I am not calling skopeo manually, but through terraform via incus provider. It runs `skopeo --insecure-policy inspect docker://docker.io/linuxserver/jellyfin` and errors out. Indeed running `skopeo --insecure-policy inspect docker://docker.io/linuxserver/jellyfin` manually gives the same error:

```
FATA[0000] Error parsing manifest for image: choosing image instance: no image found in image index for architecture "arm64", variant "v8", OS "darwin"
```

Thing is, my default_remote is set to an x86_64 machine, I am not sure if
* the inspect command is not set to the remote machine (os: incus-os, architecture: x86_64), and if it can be set
or
* the inspect command should work on macOS at all, and if not, should it be hidden 
or
* the incus provider is not setting the architecture to the remote correctly

So I am asking here and in incus to figure out where the source of the problem lies and how to solve it.

(separately, is there any way to have `skopeo inspect` on macOS just work out of the box instead of having to learn that these things need to be overridden?)

Thanks

comments.body: ok, it was in the man page, not in the --help output. I'd appreciate it being in --help as well, and off to incus to figure out why its not filtered by the remote architecture when calling skopeo

Thanks for your report. `--override-os` is visible in `skopeo --help`, but not in `skopeo inspect --help`. I don’t think we’d want to list all options in the `inspect`-specific page, but it might be worth adding a note — I don’t know whether users looking at the table of options would notice a separate text.

Changing the behavior (… to do what? always assume `linux`? somewhat plausible; always assume `amd64`? historically reasonable but increasingly problematic) would break scripts, so we can’t very well do that any more.

I wouldn't suggest changing behavior without knowing how many darwin+arm64 images exist. If it was zero, I *might* argue for falling back to another architecture by default though that just kicks the can down the line to whatever the person tries to do next with a non matching image.

I guess the weird part is that on macOS, I am using client-side tools that speak to a linux/amd64 remote, so working with metadata for non-local architecture is normal. Since lxc/podman(/docker?) use a virtual machine on macOS to run containers, maybe it is safe for skopeo to show remote machine architectures?

For immediate improvements, I think theres some more verbose error logging that could be nice for folks:
a. list hat architectures were found in the manifest
b. show how to pick one of those architectures with an example using `--override-os` etc
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.

> a. list hat architectures were found in the manifest

There are frequently quite a few, and it doesn’t solve the “how do I do this” question

> b. show how to pick one of those architectures with an example using --override-os etc

The error message comes from a library, and that library doesn’t directly know the CLI flag names.

---

I think a reasonable path forward would be to add the option to one of the examples in `inspect --help`.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
